meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Use this advanced checklist to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid deployment and monetization. Build faster, ship now.
content.answer_block: SaaS project paralysis stems from over-engineering the foundation. The solution is an opinionated starter kit that enforces constraints, automates boilerplate, and mandates immediate deployment. By integrating Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative logic, you bypass the 'blank canvas' trap and ship a revenue-ready MVP in days.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with 'perfect' codebases that never saw a single user. You wake up with a vision, spend weeks polishing the authentication flow, and eventually succumb to the crushing weight of feature creep. The momentum dies in the architecture phase, replaced by the hollow comfort of infinite refactoring.

It is time to kill the perfectionist. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you treat your project as a product, not a coding exercise. We move from artisanal development to industrial-grade assembly. With Antigravity to define the skeleton and Claude Code to drive the execution, you transition from 'aspiring builder' to 'shipped founder' in record time.

steps.title: Define the Value-First Schema
steps.description: Stop building features. Define your core data model around the specific problem your user pays to solve. Keep it lean to ensure 100% focus on the monetization path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal SaaS schema for a [Niche] application. Include only User, Subscription, and [Core Feature] tables. Focus on database normalization for rapid iteration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema based on the antigravity output'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Auth and Payment Layer
steps.description: Never write custom auth. Use your starter kit’s pre-configured Auth/Stripe integration to ensure your system is ready to collect revenue from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the authentication and Stripe webhook handlers for a Next.js starter kit. Ensure Stripe subscription states sync with user model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe webhooks and secure api routes'

steps.title: Execute Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of business logic implementation. This keeps your velocity high while maintaining strict type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for [Feature] that handles input validation and error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create service layer functions with full typescript coverage'

steps.title: Automate UI Assembly
steps.description: Use component libraries defined in your starter kit to assemble the dashboard. Focus on high-conversion UI, not aesthetic perfection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dashboard layout using Shadcn UI components for a [SaaS Type] dashboard, emphasizing data visualization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'assemble dashboard layout using existing component library'

steps.title: Automated Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Finalize your CI/CD pipeline to ensure every commit is a potential production release. Eliminate the fear of deploying code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vercel/GitHub Actions pipeline for automated testing and deployment for this SaaS starter kit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize github actions workflow for deployment'

pro_tips: Never build a landing page inside your app; keep marketing and product repos separate to avoid dependency bloat.
Force yourself to deploy on day 3, even if the app only has a sign-up form and a pricing toggle.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your checkout flow first; if the money doesn't work, the features don't matter.
Aggressively delete any code that doesn't directly contribute to the 'Aha!' moment of your user.
Monitor feature usage via analytics from day one; if a feature isn't being clicked, kill it before it drains your maintenance budget.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, stop coding. Spend 80% of your time on customer acquisition and feedback loops. Use the remaining 20% to iterate based on actual user data.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the blueprint, and the strategy to stop stalling and start shipping. The market doesn't reward perfect code; it rewards solved problems. Own your outcome. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
